heading: Withholding of charitable contributions from salaries paid by Secretary of Senate and from employees of Architect of Capitol
label: 4524 Withholding of charitable contributions from salaries paid by Secretary of Senate and from employees of Architect of Capitol

children.text: “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Senate; and
“Architect” means the Architect of the Capitol.

The Secretary and the Architect shall notify individuals whose pay is disbursed by the Secretary or who are employees of the Architect, including employees of the Botanic Garden or the Senate Restaurants of the opportunity to have amounts withheld from their pay pursuant to this section for contribution to national voluntary health and welfare agencies designated by the Director of the Office of Personnel Management pursuant to Executive Order 10927, dated .March 18, 1961

children.label: A
children.text: withhold such amount from the pay of such individual; and

children.label: B
children.text: transmit (not less than once each calendar quarter) the amount so withheld to the Combined Federal Campaign Center as specified in such request.

children.heading: Time of withholding and transmission
children.label: c Time of withholding and transmission
children.text: The Secretary and the Architect shall, to the extent practicable, carry out subsection (b) at or about the time of the Combined Federal Campaign and other fundraising in the executive branch of the Federal Government conducted pursuant to Executive Order 10927, dated , and at such other times as each such officer deems appropriate.March 18, 1961

children.label: A
children.text: the amount specified to be withheld from such pay under subsection (b) for such period; plus

children.label: B
children.text: the amount of all other withholdings from such pay for such period.

children.label: A
children.text: 50 cents, if the pay period of such individual is biweekly or semimonthly; or

children.label: B
children.text: $1, if the pay period of such individual is monthly.

children.heading: Provisions as not imposing duty, burden, requirement or penalty on United States, Senate, or any officer or employee of United States; effect of filing paper
children.label: e Provisions as not imposing duty, burden, requirement or penalty on United States, Senate, or any officer or employee of United States; effect of filing paper
children.text: This section imposes no duty, burden, or requirement upon the United States, the Senate, or any officer or employee of the United States, except as specifically provided in this section. Nothing in this section shall be deemed to consent to the application of any provision of law which has the effect of subjecting the United States, the Senate, or any officer or employee of the United States to any penalty or liability by reason of the provisions of this section. Any paper, form, document, or any other item filed with the Secretary under this section is a paper of the Senate within the provisions of rule XXX of the Standing Rules of the Senate.

children.heading: Rules and regulations
children.label: f Rules and regulations
children.text: The Secretary and the Architect are authorized to issue rules and regulations they consider appropriate in carrying out their duties under this section.
